title: 7-trial RAWM protocol
author: Teresa Macheda
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bb84iryw
materials: RAWM apparatus (circular platform, base, 8-shaped platform, 8 metal inserts, escape platform)
Spatial cues (triangle, square, checkboard pattern, cross)

steps.title: Plan Experimental Design
steps.instruction: Ensure experimental groups of mice are coded for blinding during testing. Randomize mouse test order to avoid confounded results. Conduct testing at the same time of day across all days and experiments. Test male and female mice in separate groups and cohorts, ensuring the RAWM equipment is cleaned and water changed between sexes.

steps.title: Set Up RAWM Apparatus and Mice Prep
steps.instruction: Isolate the RAWM apparatus using double black-out curtains. Inspect the pool for damage or leaks and test overhead lights 1-2 days before testing. Add the 9-cm base, 8-shaped platform, and 8 numbered metal inserts to form an 8-arm maze. Add liquid paint and refill the pool with water, ensuring arms are 9-cm above the water level to prevent mouse escape. Prepare the experimental file using tracking software and a recording sheet for environmental and animal data. Set up heating pads and clean recovery cages with paper towels, placing only half of each cage on a heating pad. Handle mice for at least 3 days before the test and mark their tails for easy identification.

steps.title: Acclimation and Pool Temperature Check
steps.instruction: On testing days, move mouse cages to the testing room and place subjects in clean, bedding-free recovery cages for at least 30 minutes to minimize stress. Ensure recovery cages have paper towels and are placed half on a heating pad for self-regulation and faster drying. Promptly replace wet paper towels. Check pool temperature (21 ± 1º C) before starting the testing procedure.

steps.title: Testing Day Structure Overview
steps.instruction: Divide mice into two cohorts (10-15 mice each). Each cohort will be tested in Block 1 (trials 1-3) first, then Block 2 (trials 4-7). A staggered design is used where one cohort completes a block before the second cohort starts the same block. For training days (Day 1 & 2), the platform is visible during Block 1 (trials 1 & 3). For testing days (Day 3 & 4), the platform is hidden for all trials. The mouse drop location varies semi-randomly.

steps.title: Training Day 1 & 2 - Block 1 (Trials 1-3)
steps.instruction:
For Cohort 1:
Trial 1 (Visible Platform): Place the flag on the platform. Gently place the mouse into the first arm facing the pool wall. The trial should last up to 60 seconds. The software automatically counts errors (incorrect arm entries, or goal arm entries without escaping). Record if the mouse spends more than 15 seconds in one place (counted as an error). Latency, distance, and speed are recorded. If the platform is not located after 1 minute, gently guide the mouse to it. Allow the mouse to stay 15 seconds on the platform (whether guided or self-escaped). Remove, gently dry, and return the mouse to its recovery cage. Continue with other mice in Cohort 1.
Trial 2 (Hidden Platform): After all Cohort 1 mice complete Trial 1, remove the flag to make the platform hidden. Repeat the procedure from Trial 1, but use a different drop arm.
Trial 3 (Visible Platform): After all Cohort 1 mice complete Trial 2, replace the flag to make the platform visible. Repeat the procedure from Trial 1, but use a different drop arm.
After Cohort 1 completes Block 1, repeat the entire Block 1 procedure for Cohort 2.

steps.title: Training Day 1 & 2 - Block 2 (Trials 4-7)
steps.instruction:
After Cohort 2 completes Block 1, Cohort 1 begins Block 2.
Trial 4 (Hidden Platform): Ensure the platform is hidden (no flag). Repeat the procedure from Trial 1 (except platform visibility), using a different drop arm.
Trial 5 (Hidden Platform): Repeat the procedure from Trial 1, using a different drop arm.
Trial 6 (Hidden Platform): Repeat the procedure from Trial 1, using a different drop arm.
Trial 7 (Hidden Platform): Repeat the procedure from Trial 1, using a different drop arm.
After Cohort 1 completes Block 2, repeat the entire Block 2 procedure for Cohort 2. Allow mice to dry off before returning them to their home cages. If fewer than 10 mice per cohort are tested, allow more resting time between blocks.

steps.title: Testing Days 3 & 4 Procedure
steps.instruction: Repeat the entire procedure from Training Days (Day 1 & 2), but ensure the platform remains hidden for all trials during Block 1 (Trials 1, 2, and 3) as well as Block 2. Allow mice to dry off before placing them back to the home cage and housing room.

steps.title: Data Collection & Error Scoring
steps.instruction: Record all data (number of errors, distance, latency, speed) using the tracking software. Score an error when the mouse enters a non-goal arm or enters the goal arm without escaping. An error is also added if the subject spends ≥ 10 seconds in the same arm or center.

steps.title: Pool Maintenance
steps.instruction: Inspect the pool for maintenance a day or few days before RAWM. Stir water daily before testing to ensure even opacity of the paint. At the end of the experiment, empty the pool and clean it first with clean water, then with a disinfecting solution. Wash and disinfect the arms and platform as well.
